---
title: "Physiological Outcomes — LMM Analysis"
subtitle: "HR and RMSSD across meditation modalities"
author: "Matteo Roberto Teagno"
description: "Master Thesis"
format:
  pdf:
    code-overflow: wrap
    geometry:
      - left=2cm
      - right=2cm
      - top=2cm
      - bottom=2cm
execute:
  message: false
  warning: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

# ============================================================
# SETUP & DATA LOADING
# ============================================================

# --- Libraries ---
library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(ggdist)
library(cowplot)
library(car)
library(effects)
library(moments)

# --- Load HR Data ---
hr <- read.csv("hr_epochs_validated.csv")

# --- Exclusions & Filtering ---
excluded_ids <- c("ID28", "ID48", "ID43", "ID10", "ID11", "ID31")

hr_clean <- hr %>%
  filter(!ID %in% excluded_ids, Valid == TRUE)

# --- Completeness Check (flag participants with < 5 epochs) ---
hr_clean %>%
  group_by(ID, Group) %>%
  summarise(
    n_epochs       = n(),
    epochs_present = paste(sort(Epoch), collapse = ","),
    .groups        = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n_epochs < 5) %>%
  print()

# --- Sample Summary ---
cat("Total participants:", n_distinct(hr_clean$ID), "\n")
cat("Total rows:",         nrow(hr_clean),           "\n")
```

```{r}
#| label: hr-analysis
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# ==============================================================================
# HR ANALYSIS — LMM
# Group × Epoch mixed effects model
# Outcome: Avg_HR
# ==============================================================================

# --- Custom group colors ---
custom_colors <- c("Control" = "grey60", "2D" = "#D4AC0D", "VR" = "#006400")

# ==============================================================================
# 1. DATA PREPARATION
# ==============================================================================
# Note: data already loaded as hr_clean in the Setup chunk above.
# Re-applying factor coding here for self-containment.

hr_clean <- hr_clean %>%
  mutate(
    Group = factor(Group, levels = c("Control", "2D", "VR")),
    ID    = as.factor(ID),
    Epoch = as.numeric(Epoch)
  )

cat("Participants included:", n_distinct(hr_clean$ID), "\n")
cat("Rows included:",         nrow(hr_clean),           "\n")
cat("\nParticipants per group:\n")
hr_clean %>% distinct(ID, Group) %>% count(Group) %>% print()

# ==============================================================================
# 2. DISTRIBUTION CHECK
# ==============================================================================

# --- Skewness ---
cat("Avg_HR skewness:", round(skewness(hr_clean$Avg_HR), 3), "\n")

# --- Shapiro-Wilk normality test ---
cat("\n--- Shapiro-Wilk: Avg_HR ---\n")
print(shapiro.test(hr_clean$Avg_HR))

# --- Visual distribution check ---
ggplot(hr_clean, aes(x = Avg_HR)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  geom_density(aes(y = after_stat(count)), color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Avg_HR") +
  theme_classic()
```
```{r}
#| label: hr-lmm
#| warning: false
#| message: false
# ==============================================================================
# 3. PRIMARY MODEL — LMM (Gaussian)
# ==============================================================================
# Fit Gaussian LMM: Group × Epoch with random intercept per participant
m_avg_hr <- lmer(Avg_HR ~ Group * Epoch + (1|ID),
                 data = hr_clean)

# --- Omnibus test (Type II ANOVA) ---
cat("\n--- LMM: Type II ANOVA table ---\n")
print(Anova(m_avg_hr, type = "II"))

# --- Full model summary ---
cat("\n--- LMM: Model summary ---\n")
print(summary(m_avg_hr))

# --- Post-hoc: pairwise group contrasts per epoch (Bonferroni-corrected) ---
cat("\n--- LMM: Post-hoc group contrasts per epoch (Bonferroni) ---\n")
emm_lmm <- emmeans(m_avg_hr, ~ Group | Epoch,
                   at = list(Epoch = c(0, 1, 2, 3, 4)))
print(pairs(emm_lmm, adjust = "bonferroni"))

# --- Estimated marginal means across epochs (collapsed across groups) ---
cat("\n--- LMM: EMMs across epochs (collapsed across groups) ---\n")
emm_epoch <- emmeans(m_avg_hr, ~ Epoch,
                     at = list(Epoch = c(0, 1, 2, 3, 4)))
print(emm_epoch)
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# 4. SENSITIVITY CHECK — GLMM (Gamma log link) vs LMM (Gaussian)
# ==============================================================================
# HR is strictly positive continuous data, making Gamma family theoretically
# appropriate. This GLMM is fitted purely as a sensitivity check to verify
# that conclusions from Model A are not an artifact of the Gaussian assumption.

# --- Fit GLMM ---
m_avg_hr_glmm <- glmer(Avg_HR ~ Group * Epoch + (1|ID),
                        data   = hr_clean,
                        family = Gamma(link = "log"))

# --- Omnibus test (Type II ANOVA) ---
cat("\n--- GLMM: Type II ANOVA table ---\n")
print(Anova(m_avg_hr_glmm, type = "II"))

# --- Full model summary ---
cat("\n--- GLMM: Model summary ---\n")
print(summary(m_avg_hr_glmm))

# --- Post-hoc: pairwise group contrasts per epoch (Bonferroni-corrected) ---
cat("\n--- GLMM: Post-hoc group contrasts per epoch (Bonferroni) ---\n")
emm_glmm <- emmeans(m_avg_hr_glmm, ~ Group | Epoch,
                     at = list(Epoch = c(0, 1, 2, 3, 4)))
print(pairs(emm_glmm, adjust = "bonferroni"))

# ==============================================================================
# MODEL COMPARISON — LMM vs GLMM
# ==============================================================================

# --- AIC comparison ---
cat("\n--- AIC comparison: LMM vs GLMM ---\n")
cat(sprintf("  LMM  (Gaussian):  AIC = %.2f\n", AIC(m_avg_hr)))
cat(sprintf("  GLMM (Gamma log): AIC = %.2f\n", AIC(m_avg_hr_glmm)))
cat("  → Lower AIC indicates better fit\n")

# --- Residual diagnostic plots (side by side) ---
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

plot(fitted(m_avg_hr), residuals(m_avg_hr),
     main = "LMM — Fitted vs. Residuals",
     xlab = "Fitted values", ylab = "Residuals")
abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)

plot(fitted(m_avg_hr_glmm), residuals(m_avg_hr_glmm, type = "pearson"),
     main = "GLMM — Fitted vs. Pearson Residuals",
     xlab = "Fitted values", ylab = "Pearson residuals")
abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```


```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# 5. VISUALIZATION — Avg_HR Raincloud Plot
# ==============================================================================
p_avg_hr <- ggplot(hr_clean %>% filter(Epoch > 0),
                   aes(x = factor(Epoch), y = Avg_HR,
                       fill = Group, color = Group)) +
  stat_halfeye(adjust = .5, width = .6, .width = 0,
               justification = -.2, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .15, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.5) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line",
               aes(group = Group), linewidth = 1,
               position = position_dodge(0.1)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point",
               aes(group = Group), size = 2,
               position = position_dodge(0.1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = custom_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  labs(title    = "Heart Rate Within Meditation Session",
       subtitle = "Heart rate decreased ~1.8 bpm across the intervention (p = .031);\nno differences between groups",
       y        = "HR (bpm)",
       x        = "Epoch") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

print(p_avg_hr)

# Save at high resolution to match the other thesis figures
ggsave("HR_raincloud.png", p_avg_hr,
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600, bg = "white")
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# DATA PREPARATION — RMSSD
# ==============================================================================

# Reload from CSV to guarantee a clean state regardless of other scripts
rmssd_raw <- read.csv("rmssd_validated.csv")

# --- Exclusions & filtering (all three groups kept) ---
rmssd_clean <- rmssd_raw %>%
  filter(!ID %in% excluded_ids, Valid == TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    Group = factor(Group, levels = c("Control", "2D", "VR")),
    ID    = as.factor(ID),
    Phase = factor(Phase, levels = c("baseline", "intervention"))
  )

cat("Participants included:", n_distinct(rmssd_clean$ID), "\n")
cat("Rows included:",         nrow(rmssd_clean),           "\n")
cat("\nParticipants per group:\n")
rmssd_clean %>% distinct(ID, Group) %>% count(Group) %>% print()

# --- Completeness check: flag participants missing a phase ---
cat("\nCompleteness check — participants with < 2 phases:\n")
rmssd_clean %>%
  group_by(ID, Group) %>%
  summarise(
    n_phases = n(),
    phases   = paste(sort(Phase), collapse = ","),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n_phases < 2) %>%
  print()
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# 10. MODEL C — RMSSD ~ Phase * Group + (1|ID)
# ==============================================================================
# RMSSD is strictly positive, making Gamma family theoretically appropriate.
# However, the GLMM with Gamma log link failed to converge on this dataset,
# producing unreliable estimates, and was therefore discarded.
# This is further supported by the Avg_HR sensitivity check, which confirmed
# that LMM and GLMM yield identical conclusions when convergence is achieved.
# Phase is a two-level factor (baseline / intervention).
# Control is the reference group (2D vs Control, VR vs Control).

# --- Fit LMM ---
m_rmssd_lmm <- lmer(RMSSD ~ Phase * Group + (1|ID),
                     data = rmssd_clean)

# --- Omnibus test (Type II ANOVA) ---
cat("\n--- Type II ANOVA table ---\n")
print(Anova(m_rmssd_lmm, type = "II"))

# --- Residual normality (post-hoc diagnostic) ---
cat("\n--- Shapiro-Wilk on residuals ---\n")
print(shapiro.test(residuals(m_rmssd_lmm)))

# --- Residual diagnostic plot ---
plot(m_rmssd_lmm, main = "Model C — Fitted vs. Residuals")

# --- Full model summary ---
cat("\n--- Model summary ---\n")
print(summary(m_rmssd_lmm))

# ==============================================================================
# POST-HOC COMPARISONS
# ==============================================================================

# --- Phase contrasts within each group (baseline vs intervention) ---
cat("\n--- Post-hoc: Phase contrasts per group (Bonferroni) ---\n")
emm_phase <- emmeans(m_rmssd_lmm, ~ Phase | Group)
print(pairs(emm_phase, adjust = "bonferroni"))

# --- Group contrasts at each phase ---
cat("\n--- Post-hoc: Group contrasts per phase (Bonferroni) ---\n")
emm_group <- emmeans(m_rmssd_lmm, ~ Group | Phase)
print(pairs(emm_group, adjust = "bonferroni"))

# --- Estimated marginal means ---
cat("\n--- Estimated marginal means ---\n")
emm_means <- emmeans(m_rmssd_lmm, ~ Group | Phase)
print(emm_means)

# --- Phase marginal means (collapsed across groups) ---
cat("\n--- Estimated marginal means per phase (all groups collapsed) ---\n")
emm_phase_collapsed <- emmeans(m_rmssd_lmm, ~ Phase)
print(emm_phase_collapsed)
print(pairs(emm_phase_collapsed, adjust = "bonferroni"))

```
```{r}
library(patchwork)
library(cowplot)

# ============================================================================
# Shared theme for raincloud plots (with border + legend off)
# ============================================================================
panel_border_theme <- theme(
  plot.background  = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
  legend.position  = "none",
  panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
)

# ============================================================================
# 1. HR PLOT (panel A) — subtitle stripped, framed
# ============================================================================
p_avg_hr <- ggplot(hr_clean %>% filter(Epoch > 0),
                   aes(x = factor(Epoch), y = Avg_HR,
                       fill = Group, color = Group)) +
  stat_halfeye(adjust = .5, width = .6, .width = 0,
               justification = -.2, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .15, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.5) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line",
               aes(group = Group), linewidth = 1,
               position = position_dodge(0.1)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point",
               aes(group = Group), size = 2,
               position = position_dodge(0.1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = custom_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  labs(title = "HR", subtitle = NULL,
       y = "HR (bpm)", x = "Epoch") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13, hjust = 0.5,
                                    margin = margin(b = 8)),
    axis.title.y     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11.5,
                                    margin = margin(r = 10)),
    axis.title.x     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11.5,
                                    margin = margin(t = 8)),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 10.5, color = "gray10"),
    axis.line        = element_line(linewidth = 0.5, color = "gray10"),
    axis.ticks       = element_line(linewidth = 0.5, color = "gray10"),
    plot.margin      = margin(15, 20, 15, 15)
  ) +
  panel_border_theme

# ============================================================================
# 2. RMSSD PLOT (panel B) — subtitle stripped, framed
# ============================================================================
p_rmssd <- ggplot(rmssd_clean,
                  aes(x = Phase, y = RMSSD,
                      fill = Group, color = Group)) +
  stat_halfeye(adjust = .5, width = .6, .width = 0,
               justification = -.2, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .15, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.5) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line",
               aes(group = Group), linewidth = 1,
               position = position_dodge(0.1)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point",
               aes(group = Group), size = 2,
               position = position_dodge(0.1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = custom_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  labs(title = "RMSSD", subtitle = NULL,
       y = "RMSSD (ms)", x = "Phase") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13, hjust = 0.5,
                                    margin = margin(b = 8)),
    axis.title.y     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11.5,
                                    margin = margin(r = 10)),
    axis.title.x     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11.5,
                                    margin = margin(t = 8)),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 10.5, color = "gray10"),
    axis.line        = element_line(linewidth = 0.5, color = "gray10"),
    axis.ticks       = element_line(linewidth = 0.5, color = "gray10"),
    plot.margin      = margin(15, 20, 15, 15)
  ) +
  panel_border_theme

# ============================================================================
# 3. CORNER BADGE HELPER
# ============================================================================
add_corner_badge <- function(plot, letter,
                             x = 0.035, y = 0.96,
                             box_w = 0.055, box_h = 0.075) {
  ggdraw(plot) +
    draw_grob(
      grid::rectGrob(
        x = x, y = y,
        width = box_w, height = box_h,
        just = c("left", "top"),
        gp = grid::gpar(fill = "white", col = "black", lwd = 0.8)
      )
    ) +
    draw_text(
      letter,
      x = x + box_w/2, y = y - box_h/2,
      hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5,
      size = 13, fontface = "bold"
    )
}

p_HR_badged    <- add_corner_badge(p_avg_hr, "A",
                                   x = 0.015, y = 0.99,
                                   box_w = 0.055, box_h = 0.075)
p_RMSSD_badged <- add_corner_badge(p_rmssd,  "B",
                                   x = 0.015, y = 0.99,
                                   box_w = 0.055, box_h = 0.075)

# ============================================================================
# 4. EXTRACT LEGEND from one of the original plots
# ============================================================================
legend_plot <- get_legend(
  p_avg_hr + theme(legend.position = "top",
                   legend.title    = element_blank(),
                   legend.text     = element_text(size = 14),     # was 11
                   legend.key.size = unit(0.9, "cm"))               # was 0.55
)

# ============================================================================
# 5. STACK — legend on top, HR + RMSSD stacked vertically
# ============================================================================
panels_col <- (p_HR_badged / p_RMSSD_badged)

combined_HRV <- wrap_plots(
  legend_plot,
  panels_col,
  ncol = 1,
  heights = c(0.06, 1)        # was c(0.04, 1)
)

combined_HRV <- combined_HRV +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "HR and RMSSD",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15,
                                            hjust = 0.5,
                                            margin = margin(b = 8)))
  )

print(combined_HRV)

# ============================================================================
# 6. SAVE
# ============================================================================
ggsave("Combined_HR_RMSSD.png", combined_HRV,
       width = 11, height = 11, dpi = 600, bg = "white")
```

```{r}

```
